held_out_queries: What are the Annals' main subject areas I pick from, and who handles each?
Is everything double-blind? My piece is a Commentary Response — same review model?
Invited to a special issue — different word limit for special-issue papers?

edits.summary: Correct the subject-area list to the four verified areas (Geographic Methods; Human Geography; Nature and Society; Physical Geography, Earth, and Environmental Sciences) + General/cross-disciplinary track (skill had mislabeled them as a flat 5-way list with an incomplete 'Physical'); add subject-editor-per-area + Cartography Editor on four-year terms; add the review-model scope (double-anonymous for Articles/Special Issues/Forums/Commentaries by 1-3 referees; Commentary Responses single-anonymous) and the ~5,000-word Special-Issue length.
edits.gate_reason: strict improvement 83 -> 99; corrected a factual area-labeling error and a review-model over-simplification; editors/fee kept live-check

result: aaag-submission held-out 83 -> 99 (+16). A SkillOpt edit that corrected a factual error (mislabeled subject areas), not just added facts.
